---
title: Troubleshooting
description: Diagnose and fix failures in the local runtime, desktop, or workspace app flow.
---

Use this page when `desktop:dev`, the embedded runtime, or a packaged runtime is not behaving the way the code says it should.

If you hit something that is not covered here, we always welcome issues. Real setup failures, unclear docs, and runtime edge cases are all useful signals for improving `holaOS` and holaOS Desktop.

## First triage commands

For the desktop dev loop, first resolve the embedded runtime port. If you want deterministic local curls, launch `desktop:dev` with `export HOLABOSS_RUNTIME_API_PORT=39160`; otherwise run the helper below and substitute the printed value as `RUNTIME_PORT`.

```bash
npm run desktop:typecheck
bash apps/desktop/scripts/check-runtime-status.sh
curl http://127.0.0.1:$RUNTIME_PORT/healthz
```

For a packaged or standalone runtime, use the port you actually bound, usually `8080`.

## `desktop:dev` exits before Electron starts

Most early failures happen in the desktop `predev` hook, before the app window exists.

- Verify that Node.js `24+` and `bun` are installed.
- Re-run `bun install` if the desktop dependency tree is incomplete. The root `npm run desktop:install` wrapper resolves to the same Bun install step.
- Check `apps/desktop/.env`. `apps/desktop/scripts/validate-dev-env.mjs` requires at least one configured remote base URL such as `HOLABOSS_BACKEND_BASE_URL`, `HOLABOSS_PROACTIVE_URL`, `HOLABOSS_CLI_PROACTIVE_URL`, or `HOLABOSS_DESKTOP_CONTROL_PLANE_BASE_URL`.
- If the failure happens during native module rebuild, re-run `bun install` and then `npm run desktop:typecheck`.

## The embedded runtime does not pass health checks

In desktop dev, the embedded runtime binds to `127.0.0.1:<derived-port>`, not a fixed `5160`. `apps/desktop/electron/main.ts` resolves that port from `HOLABOSS_RUNTIME_API_PORT` or, when unset, from a hash of the Electron `userData` path. Port `5060` is rejected.

- Run `bash apps/desktop/scripts/check-runtime-status.sh` and use the printed `runtime port` for the curls on this page.
- Check `curl http://127.0.0.1:$RUNTIME_PORT/healthz`.
- Inspect `runtime.log` under the Electron `userData` directory.
- Inspect the embedded sandbox root under `sandbox-host/`, especially `sandbox-host/state/runtime-config.json`, `sandbox-host/state/host-state.db`, and `sandbox-host/state/control-plane.db`.
- If you set a custom user-data path, make sure you are looking in `HOLABOSS_DESKTOP_USER_DATA_PATH`. Otherwise dev resolves `HOLABOSS_DESKTOP_USER_DATA_DIR` from the shell or `apps/desktop/.env`, and if that is unset it falls back to `holaboss-local-dev`.
- If you want a stable debug port, launch desktop with `HOLABOSS_RUNTIME_API_PORT=39160`.

## The runtime bundle is stale

The desktop dev loop only uses the staged bundle under `apps/desktop/out/runtime-<platform>`.

- rerun `npm run desktop:prepare-runtime:local`
- verify that `apps/desktop/out/runtime-<platform>/package-metadata.json` exists
- verify that the staged bundle contains `bin/sandbox-runtime`, `runtime/metadata.json`, and `runtime/api-server/dist/index.mjs`
- remember that `apps/desktop/scripts/watch-runtime-bundle.mjs` only rebuilds when source inputs listed in `apps/desktop/scripts/runtime-bundle-state.mjs` change

If you want to throw away the staged bundle entirely, delete `apps/desktop/out/runtime-<platform>` and rerun the staging command.

```bash
rm -rf apps/desktop/out/runtime-<platform>
npm run desktop:prepare-runtime:local
```

## The standalone or packaged runtime is on the wrong port

The launch mode controls the default port:

- embedded desktop runtime: derived `127.0.0.1:<runtime-port>` from `HOLABOSS_RUNTIME_API_PORT` or the resolved `userData` path
- packaged launcher: `8080` unless `SANDBOX_RUNTIME_API_PORT` or `SANDBOX_AGENT_BIND_PORT` overrides it
- raw `runtime/api-server/dist/index.mjs`: `3060` unless `SANDBOX_RUNTIME_API_PORT`, `SANDBOX_AGENT_BIND_PORT`, or `PORT` overrides it

Do not debug route behavior against the wrong launch mode.

## Model configuration looks correct but requests still fail

Check the provider path first:

- verify that the provider base URL is reachable
- verify that the auth token is valid
- verify that the selected model name matches the provider contract
- verify that `runtime-config.json` is being read from the expected path

The runtime config parser merges values from `runtime`, `providers.holaboss_model_proxy`, `integrations.holaboss`, and `capabilities.desktop_browser`. The executable examples for this are in `runtime/api-server/src/runtime-config.test.ts`.

If you are using direct provider fallback instead of the holaOS model proxy path, verify the corresponding direct-provider env vars such as `OPENAI_API_KEY`, `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`, or `OPENROUTER_API_KEY`.

From the Electron `userData` directory, a quick shape check looks like this:

```bash
jq '.providers.holaboss_model_proxy, .integrations.holaboss, .capabilities.desktop_browser' \
  "<userData>/sandbox-host/state/runtime-config.json"
```

## App setup fails inside an isolated app environment or container

This usually means the install step is colliding with overlay filesystem behavior.

Use the same pattern the app templates recommend:

```bash
rm -rf node_modules && npm install --maxsockets 1 && npm run build
```

Also verify that:

- `apps/<app-id>/app.runtime.yaml` declares the expected setup and start commands
- the app manifest points at the expected MCP path, if the app exposes MCP tools
- the runtime can still resolve the app's assigned ports through `/api/v1/apps/ports`
- `POST /api/v1/apps/install-archive` is not already running for the same `(workspace_id, app_id)` pair. A concurrent attempt now returns `409` with `app install already in progress for this id`.

When setup still fails, inspect the per-app setup logs first:

```bash
curl \
  "http://127.0.0.1:${RUNTIME_PORT}/api/v1/apps/my_app/setup-log?workspace_id=workspace-1"
```

This returns the tail of `<workspace>/apps/my_app/.holaboss/logs/setup.latest.log` and recent lifecycle events from `events.ndjson`.

## Workspace creation fails mid-bootstrap

Workspace creation now emits structured stage logs from Electron main with the `[holaboss.createWorkspace]` prefix.

If first-workspace onboarding appears stuck around template apply, runtime record creation, or activation, inspect those stage lines in desktop logs before changing runtime code.

## Browser tools are missing during agent runs

The browser tool surface is not always available.

- Confirm that runtime config enables the desktop browser capability and includes both the browser URL and auth token.
- Confirm that the browser work is happening in a delegated `subagent` run. In the current `pi` path, browser tools are surfaced there, while the front session usually routes browser-heavy work through delegation.
- If the runtime reports browser unavailable, inspect `runtime-config.json` and `/api/v1/runtime/status` before you patch the harness.

```bash
curl http://127.0.0.1:${RUNTIME_PORT}/api/v1/runtime/status | jq .
```

## The runtime starts but workspace data looks wrong

Check these files first:

- `workspace.yaml`
- `AGENTS.md`
- `apps/<app-id>/app.runtime.yaml`
- `.holaboss/`
- `<userData>/sandbox-host/state/host-state.db`
- `<userData>/sandbox-host/state/control-plane.db`
- `.holaboss/state/runtime.db`

If the workspace files are valid but the runtime still behaves unexpectedly, compare the current bundle against the source revision you used to stage it.

## Report an issue

If none of the fixes on this page help, submit an issue here:

- [Open a GitHub issue](https://github.com/holaboss-ai/holaOS/issues/new/choose)
